items.title: Alleged murderer 'poisoned parents to death' with insulin, court hears

children.content: Adelaide woman Raelene Polymiadis is standing trial for two counts of murder after allegedly deliberately administering "fatal doses of insulin" to her parents in separate incidents, just over a year apart.

gallery.altText: A woman wearing sunglasses walks along a footpath with a man.

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing sunglasses walks along a footpath with a man.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Dog rescued after being trapped for five days in breakwater

children.content: A 14-year-old fox terrier is back in her owner's arms after a multi-day stint trapped in the Glenelg breakwater after she broke free from her leash.

image.altText: A woman with short grey hair wearing a blue shirt and vest smiling and cuddling a small black, white and brown dog.

items.title: Charges dropped against 'masked man' after fire at popular tourism site

children.content: A man who was charged after a fire near Mount Gambier will still have to appear in court to face harm, theft and offensive language charges.

image.altText: Flames on the side of a road behind a guardrail on a sunny day

items.title: Lancaster bomber pilot and Riverland volunteer Howard Hendrick remembered

children.content: Howard Hendrick's life was dedicated to service in the Royal Australian Air Force and to his community. His contributions will forever remembered by his family and his hometown.

image.altText: Framed photos are stacked on top of each other showing a man flying a plane and getting married.

items.title: SA Police charge man over alleged possession of mobile phone gun

children.content: South Australian police have separately arrested two men for the alleged possession of a handgun disguised as a mobile phone, and a homemade "pen gun".

image.altText: A red mobile phone-looking item split in half to reveal a black trigger in the middle of the top half.

items.title: 'Smiling since last night': Community joy after Irankunda's World Cup goal

children.content: The family and community of Socceroos star Nestory Irankunda are "very emotional" after his goal-scoring World Cup debut. His cousin says he will be "forever grateful" for the support the family received after arriving in Australia, and "their blessing is seeing this at the big stage".

articles.title: Socceroos boast heavy Adelaide connection, as SA fans show support

children.content: Thousands of South Australian soccer fans descended on The Drive in Adelaide city to watch Adelaide's own Nestory Irankunda score the first half-opener, leading to Australia's win over Türkiye on Sunday.

image.altText: thousand of fans look up at a TV screen

articles.title: When Irankunda ran to the corner to emulate Cahill, it was a pre-planned tribute

children.content: Nestory Irankunda's childhood hero is none other than Tim Cahill. So it was fitting that more than 20 years after Cahill's famous celebration, this generation's Socceroos hero paid tribute.

image.altText: Nestory Irankunda punches the corner flag

articles.title: 'A footballer's dream': From humble beginnings to the world stage

children.content: The Touré brothers snuck into their local football field after hours to play the game they loved, while the Yengi brothers kicked around makeshift balls made of plastic bags. Two of them have now made it onto the world stage as Socceroos.

image.altText: composite of a Mo Toure as a young boy in a soccer field and him as an adult in a Socceroos guernsey

gallery.altText: Two soccer players in green and gold celebrate a goal

thumbnail.altText: Two soccer players in green and gold celebrate a goal

items.title: Concerns new BHP agreement 'locks in' basin water extraction

children.content: Environmentalists and First Nations groups say a new mining agreement with BHP does not do enough to end water extraction from the Great Artesian Basin. But the SA government says the agreement imposes stricter environmental regulation.

image.altText: An overhead view of a BHP wellfield in outback South Australia.

items.title: 'Lives in danger' as SA Bureau of Meteorology wave buoy fails yet again

children.content: The wave buoy off Kangaroo Island is the only such device operated by the Bureau of Meteorology in South Australia and is relied upon by professional divers and fishers.

image.altText: An orange wave buoy rider in the ocean

items.title: The multi-million-dollar tourist hotspot where locals can't afford to live

children.content: A tourist hotspot with skyrocketing rents, low rental vacancies and a dearth of public housing, has found a way to help find homes for those who can't afford to live in their own region.

image.altText: A young woman with flowing hair stands amid the leaves and flowering gumnuts of a eucalypt tree

items.title: T-Birds cap off near-flawless season with record number of wins

children.content: This year's record-breaking Adelaide Thunderbirds are shifting their focus to the Super Netball finals, with a third premiership in four years on offer.

image.altText: A Thunderbirds player and Lightning player reach across each other for the ball

items.title: Why young people say the social media ban is not working six months on

children.content: Research suggests many under-16s are still active on social media platforms. BTN High explores why and tests the accuracy of popular technology used to estimate age.

gallery.altText: A teenage boy holds up a mobile phone with the number 16 on it. Logos for Facebook, X and other platforms swirl near his face.

thumbnail.altText: A teenage boy holds up a mobile phone with the number 16 on it. Logos for Facebook, X and other platforms swirl near his face.

items.title: From wine to water, Adelaide Hills charity brings hope to kids in Kenya

children.content: From the Adelaide Hills to refugee camps in Kenya, how one young Aussie has made it his mission to help.

image.altText: A group of young boys smile and pose for a photo in rural Kenya.

items.title: SANFL players consider industrial action as hourly salary drops to $8

children.content: The SANFL Players' Association is discussing the potential of a strike in response to a 50 per cent decline in pay over the past 20 years.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie sitting on a bench in a grassed area

items.title: Gus Lamont's grandparent fined over gun offence, unrelated to 4yo's disappearance

children.content: The grandparent of missing four-year-old Gus Lamont has been fined $10,500 for possession of a gun silencer at the family's Oak Park Station, in South Australia's far north. Police have previously said the offence is not related to his disappearance.

image.altText: A person wearing with shoulder-length blonde hair wearing an Akubra hat and maroon jacket, holding a black handbag

items.title: PM to make fuel excise call early next week

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says the government will give Australians "appropriate notice" about the fuel excise rebate, as it continues to monitor the issue.

image.altText: A close up of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a white hard hat and a yellow high-vis vest.

items.title: White House UFC event ends with massive upset win in title fight

children.content: US President Donald Trump watches ring-side as the UFC event dubbed Freedom 250 ends with Justin Gaethje scoring a massive upset win over Ilia Topuria at the White House.

gallery.altText: Justin Gaethje shakes Donald Trump's hand.
A bloodied Ilia Topuria hangs his head as he is carried away from the octagon at UFC Freedom 250.
Diego Lopes stands on top of the octagon at the White House.

thumbnail.altText: Justin Gaethje shakes Donald Trump's hand.
A bloodied Ilia Topuria hangs his head as he is carried away from the octagon at UFC Freedom 250.
Diego Lopes stands on top of the octagon at the White House.

items.title: Ukrainian heritage-listed monastery severely damaged by Russian strike

children.content: Four people have been killed after a Russian strike hit a 900-year-old monastery in central Kyiv.

image.altText: An aerial shot of a golden domed monastry with fire trucks putting out a fire.

items.title: Legal bill and election breach added to minister's woes

children.content: Tasmania's racing minister is facing mounting calls to resign from cabinet, after evidence in parliament called into question what her office knew about messaging that breached election guidelines.

image.altText: A woman dressed formally looks to the distance, next to men in suits

items.title: Woman denied bail over alleged plot to smuggle fugitive bikie out of WA

children.content: Queensland woman Jessica Beckett told a West Australian court she did not know an outlaw bikie gang member was on the boat she was sailing to Indonesia.

image.altText: Two people faced blurred standing on jetty

items.title: Crown alleges man accessed $5.7m dark web bitcoin to buy home

children.content: Prosecutors allege the house Jamie Lawrence Powrie was arrested at was bought with the cryptocurrency acquired through illegal dark web activity.

image.altText: Police looking at devices

items.title: Shackleton's hut could pose explosive risk to Antarctic explorers

children.content: Chemicals found inside two of Antarctica's most important historical sites, Ernest Shackleton and Robert Falcon Scott's huts, could pose "potential explosive hazards", a report has warned.

gallery.altText: Shackleton's Nimrod Hut is nestled in the landscape at at Cape Royds, Mount Erebus is shrouded in cloud in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Shackleton's Nimrod Hut is nestled in the landscape at at Cape Royds, Mount Erebus is shrouded in cloud in the background.

items.title: Stepson of Norway's crown prince convicted of rape

children.content: Marius Borg Høiby, 29, has been found guilty of two counts of rape and other crimes.

image.altText: A young man with blond hair combed back, blue eyes and wearing a suit.

items.title: UK plans to ban social media for under-16s

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer says he will ban social media sites for under-16s and impose restrictions on gaming and live streaming platforms in some of the most far-reaching online restrictions globally.

image.altText: Keir Starmer wearing a black suit and teal tie, mid-speech while sitting at a wooden table and gesturing with his right hand.

items.title: Simple 'fakeaway' recipes for when you're on a budget

children.content: Ordering in of an evening can become a costly exercise but these five tasty recipes are sure to hit the spot.

gallery.altText: Fish burger with yoghurt tartare sauce, coleslaw and shallow-fried fish fillets, a fun family dinner.

items.title: A lost Sam Pang went looking for something. He found comedy

children.content: This workplace satire starring Sam Pang has Tasmania's first ever AFL team firmly in its sights.

image.altText: Sam Pang wears a suit and sits on a stool for a studio style portrait

items.title: History quiz: Test yourself on these past events

children.content: When was Salt Lake City awarded the XIX Winter Olympics? And how much do you know about the Fast and the Furious films? Test yourself in this week's history quiz.

image.altText: Frida Kahlo, Mexico, art, artist, anniversary, self-portrait

items.title: Clothing stunt turns into 'riot' as crowd clashes with police

children.content: Newcastle residents and authorities condemn a clothing company stunt that degenerated into a "riot" involving dozens of dirt bike riders.

gallery.altText: A groiup of young boys gathering in a park.

thumbnail.altText: A groiup of young boys gathering in a park.
Dirt bike riders wearing helmets riding around a suburban road with houses.

items.title: Icy atmosphere as Victorian MPs raise funds for MND

children.content: State government and opposition MPs take the plunge to raise tens of thousands of dollars for motor neurone disease research just days after Neale Daniher's state funeral.

image.altText: A man in a bright pink, blue, yellow and black top holds his mouth open wide with his fists in the air inside a cage.

items.title: Police uncover note, unused life jackets at scene of 'senseless' murder-suicide

children.content: Investigators are trying to determine what led to a suspected murder-suicide on Saturday, after the bodies of a father and young child were recovered from a bay off Concord.

image.altText: A boat on a dock, with police tape in front and two officers examining the scene.

items.title: Disgraced former AFL footballer jailed for pool fraud

children.content: An ex-footballer has been locked up after a jury found him guilty of defrauding multiple families with his regional pool business.

image.altText: A sketch of a bald Caucasian man in prison green

items.title: Inquest into Kumanjayi White's Alice Springs death in custody set for 2027

children.content: A four-week coronial inquest into the death of a 24-year-old man who died in a Coles supermarket in Alice Springs last year after being restrained by police officers has been set down for April 2027.

image.altText: A variety of flowers - white, yellow, plum, orange - laid on the ground at a memorial outside a supermarket.

items.title: Mother of Perth girl shot dead in Pakistan 'in extreme shock'

children.content: A nine-year-old girl fatally shot by police who were pursuing armed robbers in Pakistan is remembered as happy and bubbly by the principal of her Perth school, as her family tries to deal with their "tragic loss".

gallery.altText: Selfie of a man and his daughter on a plane.

thumbnail.altText: Selfie of a man and his daughter on a plane.

items.title: Air quality monitoring to be upgraded in the NT after Inpex under-reporting

children.content: The Northern Territory government has announced a plan to introduce new air quality monitoring of toxic pollutants, including any carcinogenic emissions. It follows a review that found gas giant Inpex had underestimated its emissions in Darwin.

image.altText: A gas plant

items.title: Major highway closure hits 100 days as business payouts slammed

children.content: Mayors want the government to provide "adequate" funding for businesses that are closing and experiencing at least 50 per cent downturns due to the failure of Victoria Pass.

image.altText: A picture of a road with no cars and closure signs
